_disclaimer: Informational estimate, not legal advice. Compensation eligibility depends on exact circumstances (extraordinary-circumstance exemptions, rerouting offered, notice given, etc). Verify with the airline or regulator before filing.

EU261.label: EU261/2004 (European Union)
EU261.appliesWhen: Departure from an EU/EEA airport on any airline, OR arrival into the EU/EEA on an EU-based airline.
EU261.reductionRule: For flights over 3500km with a delay of 3-4h (rerouted), compensation may be reduced by 50% to 300 EUR.
EU261.exemptions: Extraordinary circumstances (severe weather, air-traffic control strikes, security risks, political instability) release the airline from paying compensation, though care (meals, hotel) is still owed.

UK261.label: UK261 (Regulation (EC) 261/2004 as retained in UK law)
UK261.appliesWhen: Departure from a UK airport on any airline, OR arrival into the UK on a UK-based airline.
UK261.reductionRule: Same 50% reduction rule as EU261 for long-haul flights delayed 3-4h.

DGCA.label: DGCA CAR Section 3, Series M, Part IV (India)
DGCA.appliesWhen: Domestic Indian flights, or international flights operated by an Indian carrier or departing India.
DGCA.notes: India does not have a flat cash-for-delay rule like EU261. Compensation is mainly for denied boarding and cancellation; pure delay entitles passengers to refreshments/meals (>2h) and hotel + rebooking or full refund (>24h, or overnight delay).

cancellation.between2WeeksAnd24Hours: Alternate flight within 2h of original OR refund + compensation equal to denied-boarding amount above.
cancellation.lessThan24Hours: Alternate flight or refund + compensation equal to denied-boarding amount above.

delay.over24HoursOrOvernight: Airline must provide hotel accommodation and either rebook or fully refund.
